title: Myriopteris wrightii
taxonomy: Myriopteris wrightii is a species of fern in the family Adiantaceae. It belongs to the genus Myriopteris and is a part of the Adiantum genus's split-off in the phylogenetic sense. The plant is also known as Wright's oak fern or Wright's maidenhair fern. Myriopteris wrightii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is classified as a member of the vascular plant kingdom, specifically within the division Pteridophyta.

intro: Myriopteris wrightii, also known as Wright's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Myriopteris wrightii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, and it is also used in tincture form to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
